metadata.source_paper: Surono, M. (2012). Tectonostratigraphy of the eastern part of Sulawesi, Indonesia: in relation to the terrane origins. Jurnal Sumber Daya Geologi, 22(4), 197–210.
metadata.region: East and Southeast Arms Sulawesi — terrane structure
metadata.comprehension_summary: Tectonostratigraphic synthesis of Eastern Sulawesi identifying 3 provinces: (1) Western Volcanic Arc (W Sulawesi), (2) Eastern Sulawesi Ophiolite Belt (ESO — mid-oceanic ridge origin, ~17-24°S palaeolatitude, formed Late Cretaceous ~93-79 Ma, obducted onto continental terranes Early Oligocene-Middle Miocene ~33-26 Ma), (3) Continental fragments from Australian Continent (SE Arm/East Arm: Meluhu Fm Triassic ~20°S, similar to Kubor Anticline PNG; Banggai-Sula similar to N margin Australia). Molasse deposited Early Miocene-Pliocene post-collision.
main_conclusions: ESO formed in mid-oceanic ridge at ~17-24°S palaeolatitude (within Pacific Plate), Late Cretaceous
ESO obducted onto continental terranes during Early Oligocene-Middle Miocene (~33-26 Ma based on melange dates)
Continental terranes of Australian Continent origin: Meluhu Fm deposited at ~20°S, Late Triassic
Banggai-Sula stratigraphy correlates with Kubor Anticline, N margin Australia
ESO palaeolatitude 17-24°S from radiolarian dating (Valanginian-Early Cretaceous pelagic cover) and palaeomagnetic data
60° clockwise rotation of ESO block post-Cretaceous (palaeomagnetic, Mubroto 1988)
Sulawesi molasse basin: Early Miocene-Pliocene, unconformable on collision complex

properties.name: East Sulawesi Ophiolite Belt (ESO) — síntesis tectonoestratigráfica
properties.full_name: Eastern Sulawesi Ophiolite Belt — mid-oceanic ridge origin, ~17-24°S palaeolatitude, Late Cretaceous–Early Tertiary
properties.obduction_age: Early Oligocene-Middle Miocene (~33-26 Ma from melange dates)
properties.obduction_target: Continental terranes of Australian origin (SE and East Arms)
properties.paper_finding: ESO formed at 17-24°S palaeolatitude in Pacific Plate mid-oceanic ridge; obducted onto Australian-derived terranes in Early Oligocene-Middle Miocene; subsequent 60° clockwise rotation
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono regional ESO sobre el brazo este de Sulawesi. Coincide con canon_eso ya existente; este feature aporta contexto temporal y de origen.

conclusions.text: ESO formed in mid-oceanic ridge at ~17-24°S (Pacific Plate), Late Cretaceous; obducted onto Australian-derived continental terranes ~33-26 Ma (Early Oligocene-Middle Miocene)
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El ESO viajó desde latitudes del Pacífico S (~20°S) hasta su posición actual en el brazo este de Sulawesi — implica desplazamiento ~2000 km durante ~60-70 Ma de deriva antes de la obducción

properties.name: Banggai-Sula Microcontinent — afinidad australiana
properties.full_name: Banggai-Sula Microcontinent — Australian Continent origin, similar to Kubor Anticline PNG, N margin Australia
properties.basement_description: Mesozoic sedimentary sequence (Tampakura/Tobelo/Salodik formations), Triassic continental clastics
properties.palaeogeographic_analogue: Kubor Anticline, N margin Papua New Guinea
properties.collision_timing: Late Neogene (Late Oligocene-Middle Miocene for ESO obduction; Late Miocene-Pliocene for final collision)
properties.paper_finding: Banggai-Sula stratigraphy (Eo-Oligocene Tampakura Fm, Triassic continental clastics) correlates with Kubor Anticline PNG and N margin Australia — confirms Australian-derived origin
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono regional Banggai-Sula incluyendo las islas. Límites aproximados basados en descripción general del paper.

conclusions.text: Banggai-Sula stratigraphy (Tampakura Fm Eo-Oligocene + Triassic clastics) is correlatable with Kubor Anticline PNG — both derived from N margin of Australian Continent
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Confirma el origen australiano del microcontinente Banggai-Sula — implica que la colisión con el brazo este de Sulawesi fue una colisión continente-continente, no oceánica

properties.name: SE Arm Continental Terrane (Meluhu) — afinidad australiana
properties.full_name: Southeast Arm Continental Terrane — Meluhu Formation (Late Triassic), Australian Continent origin at ~20°S palaeolatitude
properties.key_unit: Meluhu Formation (Late Triassic continental clastics + reefal limestones, deposited ~20°S)
properties.palaeogeographic_analogue: N margin Australian Continent, Late Triassic
properties.paper_finding: Meluhu Fm palaeoclimate (warm, high rainfall) and palaeolatitude (~20°S) consistent with Late Triassic N margin of Australian Continent; Early Jurassic separation from Gondwana
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono aproximado del brazo SE de Sulawesi, cubriendo el área de distribución de la Formación Meluhu y terrenos continentales asociados.

conclusions.text: SE Arm continental terrane (Meluhu Fm) deposited at ~20°S palaeolatitude during Late Triassic on N margin of Australian Continent; separated Early Jurassic
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El brazo SE de Sulawesi es un fragmento continental australiano separado en el Jurásico Temprano — la colisión con Sulawesi en el Neógeno fue continental y no oceánica
